domain: arboriculture (Reineke self-thinning rule for forest stands)
specific_mechanism: Reineke's rule: maximum stand density N_max and mean diameter D obey log(N_max) = -1.605 * log(D) + k. As a stand ages, competition forces the (N, D) trajectory to track the frontier in log-log space — the stand traverses the null-space of (compute = light × water × N × D) by trading density for size.
llm_application: Transformer self-thinning attention: at runtime, prune low-utility attention heads and re-allocate the freed KV-cache to surviving heads' effective rank, such that the (active_head_count H, per-head effective rank r) state traverses a fixed log(H) + 1.6 * log(r) ≈ const frontier as context length grows. Replaces fixed (H, r) with a dynamic null-space-traversal constraint.
novelty_claim: Published 2024-2026 LLM head-pruning work uses head importance scores to drop heads; this candidate proposes the additional constraint that surviving heads' rank GROWS along a fixed slope-1.6 log-log frontier matching Reineke's frontier slope, not just that head count decreases. Whether this exact slope-coupled constraint is in the literature is the open question.
